scenes.narration: HashMap O(1) hai? Bhai, worst case O(N) ho sakta hai — collision strategy galat samjha.

Mistake: HashMap ko constant-time samjh ke deep dive nahi kiya. Worst case sab keys same bucket me — O(N) lookup. Java 8 me chain length 8 cross hua to red-black tree me convert hota hai — O(log N) worst case. Ye exact line interview me poochi jaati hai.

Library shelves soch — har shelf ek bucket. Hash function decide karta hai book kis shelf pe. Same shelf pe multiple books = collision. Chaining matlab us shelf pe linked-list rakhna. Open addressing matlab agla shelf try karna. Load factor 0.75 cross hua to library expand kar — double shelves.

Java HashMap: array of buckets, default 16, load factor 0.75. hash(key) = key.hashCode() XOR (hashCode >>> 16) — high bits ko low bits me mix. Bucket index = hash & (n-1). Collision pe linked list, length ≥ 8 to TreeNode (red-black tree). Resize: 2x array, all entries rehash.

Treeification threshold 8, untreeify 6, min-tree-capacity 64. Resize O(N) but amortized O(1). Hash distribution important — bad hashCode (e.g. always 0) defeats everything. equals() + hashCode() contract: equal objects must have equal hashCodes. ConcurrentHashMap uses CAS + segment locks for thread-safety.

Java 7 HashMap ek classic CVE tha — DoS via hash collisions, attacker same-bucket keys send karta tha O(N^2) processing. Java 8 me red-black tree fallback aur randomized hash seed se fix hua. Production grade Map: Caffeine cache uses W-TinyLFU + similar internals.
Interview line: HashMap = array + chaining + load factor 0.75 + treeify at 8 (Java 8) + resize 2x. hashCode/equals contract mandatory. Worst case O(log N) post-Java-8.
